name: TorchIO (Medical Image Preprocessing for Deep Learning)

performance.notes: SimpleITK/ITK backend; MRI resampling and augmentation CPU-bound

agent_readiness.notes: Medical image preprocessing and augmentation for PyTorch. import torchio as tio. subject = tio.Subject(mri=tio.ScalarImage('brain.nii.gz'), label=tio.LabelMap('seg.nii.gz')). transform = tio.Compose([tio.RescaleIntensity(out_min_max=(0, 1)), tio.RandomFlip(axes=(0,)), tio.RandomNoise(), tio.RandomAffine()]). dataset = tio.SubjectsDataset(subjects, transform=transform). Preprocessing: Resample, ZNormalization, HistogramStandardization. Augmentation: RandomMotion, RandomGhosting, RandomBias, RandomElasticDeformation. NIFTI, DICOM, MHA format support.

gotchas: Highly domain-specific — only useful for 3D medical image deep learning
3D augmentation is much slower than 2D albumentations
DICOM loading requires additional setup compared to NIFTI
best_when: 3D MRI/CT segmentation and classification with PyTorch
Medical image augmentation for domain-specific deep learning
summary: TorchIO provides medical image preprocessing and augmentation for PyTorch 3D deep learning. Subject(mri=ScalarImage('brain.nii.gz')). Transforms: Resample, ZNormalization, RandomMotion, RandomElasticDeformation, RandomNoise. SubjectsDataset for DataLoader integration. NIFTI/DICOM/MHA support. Apache-2.0. Best for brain/organ segmentation research. For 2D medical images use albumentations; for full medical DL framework use MONAI.
